title: refactor(claw-interface): retype Account.openclaw_app to OpenClawAppRecord (#1593)
message:
refactor(claw-interface): retype Account.openclaw_app to OpenClawAppRecord (#1593)

## Summary

Advances the typed-Account migration started by PR #1574 (reads) and
#1585 (writes) — `Account.openclaw_app` and `AccountUpdate.openclaw_app`
are now typed `OpenClawAppRecord | None` instead of `dict | None`. The
two consumers that already accessed `account.openclaw_app` directly
(`bot_resources.py`, `openclaw_conversation.py`) move to typed attribute
access; the `ensure_app` write tightens to pass `OpenClawAppRecord`
straight into `AccountUpdate` (one fewer `model_dump` cycle).

## What changed

**Schema** (`app/schema/`):
| File:line | Change |
|---|---|
| `openclaw.py:287-303` | `OpenClawAppRecord.app_id` loosened from
`Field(..., …)` to `Field(default="", …)` so partial production docs and
40+ legacy fixtures validate. All four fields switched to keyword form —
positional `Field("", …)` trips pyright's `reportCallIssue` on
partial-kwarg construction. Docstring updated to record the WHY
(`ensure_app` already does `result.get("id", "")`). |
| `account.py:14, 257-265, 365` | New `from app.schema.openclaw import
OpenClawAppRecord` import. Both `Account.openclaw_app` and
`AccountUpdate.openclaw_app` retyped from `dict \| None` →
`OpenClawAppRecord \| None`. Replaced the "stay as raw dicts" block with
a single note that `openclaw_bots` is still next-phase. |

**Typed-attribute consumers**:
| File:line | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/routes/openclaw_conversation.py:48-51` | `app.get("api_token")` /
`app["api_token"]` → `app.api_token` |
| `app/services/bot_resources.py:80-89` | Same retype on the
bot-resource sync path |

**Write site**:
| File:line | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/services/openclaw/bot_lifecycle.py:182` |
`AccountUpdate(openclaw_app=app_record.model_dump(), …)` →
`AccountUpdate(openclaw_app=app_record, …)`. Pydantic accepts the typed
instance without re-validating; the repo's single
`model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` produces the Mongo `$set` payload — one
fewer dump cycle. |

**Tests**:
| File:line | Change |
|---|---|
| `tests/unit/test_account_openclaw_app_typed.py` | New file, 12 tests:
dict-input coercion, partial-doc tolerance (the load-bearing case for
the `app_id` default loosening), record-instance acceptance,
`model_dump()` roundtrip-to-dict (so legacy `user.get('openclaw_app')`
consumers via `account.model_dump()` keep working), `AccountUpdate` with
`exclude_unset=True` behavior including the explicit-`None`-for-clear
path used by `clear_openclaw_state`. |
| `tests/bdd/step_defs/test_user_repo.py:152` | Migrated
`AccountUpdate(openclaw_app={"app_id": …})` to
`AccountUpdate(openclaw_app=OpenClawAppRecord(app_id=…))` — keeps the
BDD step type-safe end-to-end. |

## Why loosen `app_id`?

`OpenClawAppRecord.app_id` used to be `Field(...)` (required). 40+
existing test fixtures shape `openclaw_app` as `{"api_token": "tok"}`
with no `app_id`, and the production write path at
`bot_lifecycle.py:174` is already defaulted: `app_id=result.get("id",
"")`. Business logic only truthy-checks `app_id` (e.g. `ensure_app` line
156: `if app and app.get("app_id") and app.get("api_token")`). Loosening
to `default=""` aligns the schema contract with actual usage and avoids
a 40-fixture rewrite.

## Why keep dict-shape consumers working?

~40 call sites still do `user = account.model_dump();
user.get("openclaw_app")`. Pydantic's nested-model serialization
roundtrips `OpenClawAppRecord` → dict on `model_dump()`, so those call
sites continue to work unchanged. This keeps the migration
**incremental** — no need to touch every dict-shape consumer in this PR.

## Verification

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `pyright app/ tests/` | 0 errors |
| `ruff check app/ tests/` | All checks passed |
| `pytest --cov=app -q` | 3443 passed, 2 pre-existing failures in
`tests/unit/test_ci_lint_deptry.py` (devcontainer git-worktree path
resolution; reproduces on HEAD pre-change). **Total coverage 90%**,
meets `--cov-fail-under=90`. |

## Test plan
- [x] `pyright app/ tests/` — 0 errors
- [x] `ruff check app/ tests/` — clean
- [x] `pytest --cov=app -q` — 3443 pass, 90% coverage
- [ ] CI `python-code-quality / build-and-test`
- [ ] CI `auto-review`
- [ ] CI `check-pr-title`

Business logic only truthy-checks `app_id` (e.g. `ensure_app` line 156: `if app and app.get("app_id") and app.get("api_token")`). Loosening to `default=""` aligns the schema contract with actual usage and avoids a 40-fixture rewrite.

## Why keep dict-shape consumers working?

This keeps the migration **incremental** — no need to touch every dict-shape consumer in this PR.

## Verification

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `pyright app/ tests/` | 0 errors |
| `ruff check app/ tests/` | All checks passed |
| `pytest --cov=app -q` | 3443 passed, 2 pre-existing failures in `tests/unit/test_ci_lint_deptry.py` (devcontainer git-worktree path resolution; reproduces on HEAD pre-change). **Total coverage 90%**, meets `--cov-fail-under=90`. |

## Test plan
- [x] `pyright app/ tests/` — 0 errors
- [x] `ruff check app/ tests/` — clean
- [x] `pytest --cov=app -q` — 3443 pass, 90% coverage
- [ ] CI `python-code-quality / build-and-test`
- [ ] CI `auto-review`
- [ ] CI `check-pr-title`

title: feat(web): auto-redirect logged-in users from landing to chat (#1589)
message:
feat(web): auto-redirect logged-in users from landing to chat (#1589)

Logged-in users visiting the root path (/ or /zh) were shown the landing
page instead of being redirected. Now:
- Authenticated + ?redirect=/xxx → redirect to that path
- Authenticated + no params → redirect to /chat
- Unauthenticated → stay on landing, redirect after login

Extracts redirect logic into useLandingRedirect hook with open-redirect
validation and URL cleanup.
author: bill-srp
pr_title: feat(web): auto-redirect logged-in users from landing to chat
pr_body:
Logged-in users visiting the root path (/ or /zh) were shown the landing page instead of being redirected. Now:
- Authenticated + ?redirect=/xxx → redirect to that path
- Authenticated + no params → redirect to /chat
- Unauthenticated → stay on landing, redirect after login

Extracts redirect logic into useLandingRedirect hook with open-redirect validation and URL cleanup.

title: fix(web): skip onboarding entirely after recreate (#1595)
message:
fix(web): skip onboarding entirely after recreate (#1595)

## Summary
- **Bug**: Recreate Claw 后用户被强制走 onboarding 流程（name/role 步骤），但 recreate
是老用户重建机器人，不应再走 onboarding
- **Fix**: 移除 recreate 流程中的 `ONBOARDING_RECREATE` 标志和 progress
重置逻辑，依赖现有的 `hasBoundInviteCode` 检查自然跳过 onboarding，聊天页自行处理 bot 初始化状态
- 同时还原 `resolveStatus.ts` 的 reOnboarding 分支（recreate 不再触发它），还原对应测试

Follow-up to #1587

## Test plan
- [ ] 用已完成 onboarding 的账号登录
- [ ] 进入聊天页 → 设置 → Advanced Options → Recreate Claw
- [ ] 验证 reload 后不显示任何 onboarding 步骤，直接进入聊天页
- [ ] 验证新用户首次 onboarding 流程不受影响

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: tim-srp
pr_title: fix(web): skip onboarding entirely after recreate
pr_body:
## Summary
- **Bug**: Recreate Claw 后用户被强制走 onboarding 流程（name/role 步骤），但 recreate 是老用户重建机器人，不应再走 onboarding
- **Fix**: 移除 recreate 流程中的 `ONBOARDING_RECREATE` 标志和 progress 重置逻辑，依赖现有的 `hasBoundInviteCode` 检查自然跳过 onboarding，聊天页自行处理 bot 初始化状态
- 同时还原 `resolveStatus.ts` 的 reOnboarding 分支（recreate 不再触发它），还原对应测试

Follow-up to #1587

## Test plan
- [ ] 用已完成 onboarding 的账号登录
- [ ] 进入聊天页 → 设置 → Advanced Options → Recreate Claw
- [ ] 验证 reload 后不显示任何 onboarding 步骤，直接进入聊天页
- [ ] 验证新用户首次 onboarding 流程不受影响

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: fix(openclaw): /init pre-claim mutex (ECA-655) (#1594)
message:
fix(openclaw): /init pre-claim mutex (ECA-655) (#1594)

## Summary

- Adds an atomic pre-claim mutex (`openclaw_bot_creating` timestamp on
the account) so concurrent `/init` calls don't both reach OpenClaw with
the same deterministic slug `zoo-<uid8>-<int(time.time())>`. Same-second
races were causing HTTP 400 `slug is already taken` (Linear ECA-655).
- The claim is acquired atomically before any external side effect (MM
provision, OpenClaw POST), released conditionally on the request's own
claim timestamp so a stalled request can't clobber a newer owner's lock,
and TTL-bounded at 30s so a crashed request self-heals.
- Extracts two typed helpers — `user_repo.claim_bot_creating` /
`release_bot_creating` — to keep the raw mongo CAS dicts out of service
code (matches existing `orders_repo.claim_entitlement_lock` pattern).

## Background

ECA-655 reported `[OPENCLAW] Create bot HTTP 400 -
body={"code":400,"message":"slug is already taken"}` from prod logs. The
three pre-existing layers of defense all missed the case:

| Layer | Why it missed |
|---|---|
| Frontend `initInFlightRef` / `silentRetryInFlightRef` | per-mount;
cross-tab / HMR / Strict-Mode double-mount slips through |
| Rate limit (30 calls / 5s) | not a mutex |
| Mongo CAS at end of `_create_and_start_bot` | runs *after* the
OpenClaw POST — second concurrent OpenClaw call has already been
rejected by the time mongo serializes |

Fix: hoist the CAS to *before* any external side effect.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit: new `test_pre_claim_failure_skips_openclaw_create` — losing
concurrent claimant returns `waiting` without calling
`client.create_bot`
- [x] Unit: new `test_releases_claim_when_create_bot_fails` — non-401
failure releases the claim AND the release query is guarded by the claim
timestamp (Codex P2)
- [x] Unit: new `test_releases_claim_when_client_construction_fails` —
`get_openclaw_client()` raising still releases (Codex P3)
- [x] `tests/unit/test_openclaw_routes.py`: 67 passed
- [x] `tests/unit/test_user_repo.py`: 30 passed
- [x] ruff + pyright clean
- [x] TDD discipline: each new test verified RED → GREEN (release block
temporarily disabled to confirm tests catch the regression)
- [x] Codex review pass: 2 findings (P2 ownership guard on release, P3
client construction inside try) applied
author: kaka-srp
pr_body:
## Summary

- Adds an atomic pre-claim mutex (`openclaw_bot_creating` timestamp on the account) so concurrent `/init` calls don't both reach OpenClaw with the same deterministic slug `zoo-<uid8>-<int(time.time())>`. Same-second races were causing HTTP 400 `slug is already taken` (Linear ECA-655).
- The claim is acquired atomically before any external side effect (MM provision, OpenClaw POST), released conditionally on the request's own claim timestamp so a stalled request can't clobber a newer owner's lock, and TTL-bounded at 30s so a crashed request self-heals.
- Extracts two typed helpers — `user_repo.claim_bot_creating` / `release_bot_creating` — to keep the raw mongo CAS dicts out of service code (matches existing `orders_repo.claim_entitlement_lock` pattern).

## Background

ECA-655 reported `[OPENCLAW] Create bot HTTP 400 - body={"code":400,"message":"slug is already taken"}` from prod logs. The three pre-existing layers of defense all missed the case:

| Layer | Why it missed |
|---|---|
| Frontend `initInFlightRef` / `silentRetryInFlightRef` | per-mount; cross-tab / HMR / Strict-Mode double-mount slips through |
| Rate limit (30 calls / 5s) | not a mutex |
| Mongo CAS at end of `_create_and_start_bot` | runs *after* the OpenClaw POST — second concurrent OpenClaw call has already been rejected by the time mongo serializes |

Fix: hoist the CAS to *before* any external side effect.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit: new `test_pre_claim_failure_skips_openclaw_create` — losing concurrent claimant returns `waiting` without calling `client.create_bot`
- [x] Unit: new `test_releases_claim_when_create_bot_fails` — non-401 failure releases the claim AND the release query is guarded by the claim timestamp (Codex P2)
- [x] Unit: new `test_releases_claim_when_client_construction_fails` — `get_openclaw_client()` raising still releases (Codex P3)
- [x] `tests/unit/test_openclaw_routes.py`: 67 passed
- [x] `tests/unit/test_user_repo.py`: 30 passed
- [x] ruff + pyright clean
- [x] TDD discipline: each new test verified RED → GREEN (release block temporarily disabled to confirm tests catch the regression)
- [x] Codex review pass: 2 findings (P2 ownership guard on release, P3 client construction inside try) applied

title: feat(web): Landing-page auto-hire and initial-query delivery flow (#1584)
message:
feat(web): Landing-page auto-hire and initial-query delivery flow (#1584)

## Summary

Implements the chat-side of
[ECA-641](https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-641/web-specialist-落地页用户信息延续与初始-query-投递):
when a user arrives at `/chat?sp=<specialist_id>` from a Specialist
landing page with matching localStorage context, the chat client
auto-hires the specialist (if needed), switches to it, and delivers the
initial query — all gated behind auth + onboarding + chat-ready, with an
explicit Main-Agent fallback for failure paths.

The producer side (landing-page `writeLandingContext` caller, E1–E9
instrumentation, send success/failure telemetry) is out of scope for
this PR — see [Deferred](#deferred) below.

## Files

- **`web/src/lib/landing-context.ts`** — context schema + storage
helpers
- `localStorage` (not session) so context survives any new-tab/window
hop in the auth flow per ECA-641 §2's "全程延续" target
- 24h TTL via `created_at` field — drops zombie contexts from abandoned
flows; missing `created_at` is treated as fresh for backwards-compat
with older landing-page writes
- Cross-validates URL `?sp=` against stored `specialist_id` (mismatch →
discard)
- Sanitizes nested fields (`initial_query.text`, `source`, `created_at`)
on read — localStorage is user-tamperable, so a malformed payload like
`{ initial_query: { text: 123 } }` is coerced to `{ text: '' }` instead
of crashing Phase 5 at `.trim()`
- Idempotency via `markLandingDelivered(landing_session_id)` — also in
localStorage so the marker follows the user across tabs

- **`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/hooks/useLandingContextFlow.ts`** —
6-phase state machine
  ```
idle → awaiting_auth → awaiting_chat_ready → hiring → delivering →
completed
  ```
- **Phase 3 catalog gates** — holds while `catalogLoading`; holds while
`catalogItems.length === 0` (`useOfficialAgentCatalog` swallows fetch
errors and surfaces them as `items=[] + isLoading=false`,
indistinguishable from a genuinely-empty catalog at read time); **Phase
3b** schedules an 8s safety-net timeout so we don't hang forever on a
persistent catalog outage. Mirrors `useDeepLinkHireFlow`'s pattern.
- **Phase 4 hire** — `installOpenClawAgentAsync` +
`waitForOpenClawAgentOperation` are part of the hire transaction;
`refreshUserAgentsCache` is wrapped in its own try/catch so a transient
`/users/me/agents` GET failure doesn't get misclassified as install
failure (would otherwise route the prompt to Main Agent even though the
specialist IS hired server-side).
- **Phase 5 delivery gate** — `currentAgentId !== state.resolvedAgentId`
blocks delivery until `router.replace` propagates and
`handleSendMessage`'s closure rebuilds with the new
`agentId`/`sessionKey`. Treating `null` as "main agent" on both sides
means fallback paths also wait for the URL to switch before sending.
- **ECA-641 §4.2 Main-Agent fallback** — fallback paths (specialist not
in catalog, hire failure, catalog-empty timeout) all call
`onSwitchAgent(null)` explicitly, which routes through
`router.replace('/chat')`. This both routes the user to a working chat
surface AND strips `?sp=` from the URL.
- **Best-effort delivery** — both `.then` and `.catch` branches mark
delivered + clear context. Intentional: `handleSendMessage` swallows
errors and surfaces them via toast, so the auto-flow doesn't retry; the
user manually retries via the chat input. Prevents retry loops in
degenerate failure modes.
- **Latched `canUseChatEver` / `isChatReadyEver`** — both signals
oscillate during post-login resolution and may never both be `true` at
the same instant; latching converts the gate to a monotonic "has been
true at least once this session" predicate. Mirrors
`useDeepLinkHireFlow`.

- **`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/GenClawClient.tsx`** — integration
- Wires the hook with `searchParams`, `agentId` (as `currentAgentId`),
and existing handlers
- `handleLandingSwitchAgent(agentId | null)` — `null` ⇒ Main Agent ⇒
`router.replace('/chat')`; non-null ⇒
`router.replace('/chat?agent_id=...')`. Either way the entire query
string is overwritten, so `?sp=` is stripped on completion.
- Coexists with existing `useDeepLinkHireFlow` (different URL param:
`sp` vs `agent_id`).

## ECA-641 spec coverage

| Spec section | Status |
|---|---|
| §1 specialist_id + initial_query.text + source + landing_session_id |
✅ |
| §1 initial_query.attachments | ⏭️ Embedded as URLs in `text` per
direction (no separate field; chat input renders markdown) |
| §2 cross-stage persistence (登录前 / 注册中 / 注册后 / Onboarding) | ✅
localStorage + 24h TTL |
| §3.1 Specialist hire prerequisite | ✅ Phase 4 |
| §3.2 Auto-land on Specialist chat | ✅ `onSwitchAgent` + URL switch |
| §3.3 Auto-send initial query | ✅ Phase 5 |
| §3.3 Idempotency (refresh / re-click / reconnect) | ✅
`landing_session_id` + `markLandingDelivered` |
| §4.1 No-query, specialist alive | ✅ Phase 5 empty-text branch (no
send, still lands on chat page) |
| §4.2 Specialist missing / unavailable / hire failure | ✅ Explicit
`onSwitchAgent(null)` on every fallback path |
| Defensive: malformed localStorage shape | ✅ Read-time sanitization |
| Defensive: catalog fetch failure | ✅ 8s timeout fallback |
| Defensive: `refreshUserAgentsCache` transient failure | ✅ Isolated;
doesn't trigger main-agent fallback |

## Deferred

These ECA-641 requirements are NOT in this PR — tracked separately:

- **Producer side (`writeLandingContext` caller)** — landing page must
write context on CTA click. No caller currently in `web/src/`; landing
page lives in a different bundle/repo or is unwired.
- **E1–E9 instrumentation** — 9 events with `landing_session_id`
correlation key. Hook has the data; emission needs the events lib wired.
- **Send success/failure telemetry (E7 `initial_query_sent` with
`send_success` / `error_type`)** — `handleSendMessage` currently
swallows errors and shows a toast; needs a contract change to return
success status without breaking call sites that depend on the
swallow-and-toast behavior.

## Test plan

CI gates (all green on `8d059dc0b`):
- ✅ `web-quality / web-quality` (lint + tsc + vitest, 305 tests
including 17 new in `useLandingContextFlow.unit.spec.ts` and 23 in
`landing-context.unit.spec.ts`)
- ✅ `auto-review / auto-review`
- ✅ `codex-review`

Manual smoke (recommended before merge):
- [ ] Land on `/chat?sp=<known_specialist>` with seeded localStorage
context → specialist auto-hired, URL becomes `?agent_id=<sp>`, initial
query appears as a sent user message in that specialist's chat
- [ ] Revisit same URL after delivery → idempotency guard skips re-send
(no duplicate message)
- [ ] `?sp=<unknown_specialist>` (in catalog but not hireable / hire
fails) → URL becomes `/chat` (no agent_id, `?sp=` stripped), Main Agent
receives the prompt
- [ ] `?sp=<X>` with empty/whitespace `initial_query.text` → user lands
on Specialist chat, no message sent
- [ ] Open landing-context tab, then a 2nd tab on same origin during
flow → only one delivers; the other reads delivered marker and skips
- [ ] Tamper localStorage with `{ initial_query: { text: 123 } }` →
context discarded, no crash

## Landing-page integration guide

> This is what a Specialist landing page needs to do for the auto-flow
to pick up its context.

### TL;DR

On the CTA click that sends the user to ZooClaw:

1. Write a `LandingContext` to `localStorage` under the key
`ecap:landingContext`.
2. Navigate to `/chat?sp=<specialist_id>` on the same origin.

That's it. The chat client handles everything else (auth gating, hire,
switch, deliver, cleanup).

### Schema

```ts
// Same-origin write — the chat client reads back from localStorage.
const ctx = {
  specialist_id: 'sp-resume-coach',          // required, must match ?sp= in URL
  initial_query: {
    text: 'Help me draft a resume for ...',  // required, even if empty
                                              // attachments: embed as URLs in `text`
                                              // (markdown image syntax / plain links — chat input renders both)
  },
  source: 'landing-resume-coach-v1',         // free-form; appears in telemetry
  landing_session_id: crypto.randomUUID(),   // MUST be unique per CTA click — drives idempotency
  created_at: Date.now(),                    // epoch ms; 24h TTL applies
}

localStorage.setItem('ecap:landingContext', JSON.stringify(ctx))
window.location.assign('/chat?sp=sp-resume-coach')
```

### Field-by-field contract

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `specialist_id` | string | ✅ | Must equal the `?sp=` query param on
the destination URL. Mismatch → context discarded. |
| `initial_query.text` | string | ✅ | The full prompt to auto-send.
Variable substitution is **landing-side only** — the chat client
receives it as a finished string. Empty string is allowed: the user
lands on the Specialist chat with no auto-send. |
| `source` | string | recommended | Free-form identifier for the landing
page / variant. Surfaced in telemetry events (E1–E9). Use stable
identifiers like `landing-resume-coach-v1`, not URLs. |
| `landing_session_id` | string | ✅ | Must be **unique per CTA click**.
Drives idempotency: the same `landing_session_id` will only deliver once
across refreshes / re-clicks / reconnects. Use `crypto.randomUUID()` or
a server-generated UUID. **Do NOT reuse** the user's session ID, account
ID, or anything else with non-1:1 cardinality to the click event. |
| `created_at` | number | ✅ | Epoch ms (`Date.now()`). Used as a 24h TTL
safety net to drop zombie contexts from abandoned flows. Stale entries
are silently dropped on read. |

### Attachments

Out of scope for this PR's schema. **Embed as URLs in the `text` field**
— the chat input renders markdown image syntax (`![alt](https://...)`)
inline and shows other URLs as previewable links. If you need a true
file attachment with metadata, that's a follow-up; for now URLs are the
path.

### Behavior the chat client handles for you

- ✅ Gates on auth + onboarding + WebSocket-ready before doing anything
- ✅ Hires the specialist if not already hired (auto-installs, waits for
completion, refreshes user-agents cache)
- ✅ Switches the chat URL to `?agent_id=<specialist_id>` (and strips
`?sp=`) before sending
- ✅ Auto-sends `initial_query.text` as a normal user message in that
specialist's chat
- ✅ Marks the `landing_session_id` delivered in `localStorage` so
refresh / re-click is a no-op
- ✅ Falls back to Main Agent if specialist is missing from catalog, hire
fails, or catalog fetch persistently fails (8s timeout)
- ✅ Clears `ecap:landingContext` after the flow completes — landing page
does NOT need to clean up

### What landing-page code should NOT do

- ❌ Don't read or clear `ecap:landingContext` after writing it — that's
the chat client's job.
- ❌ Don't reuse a `landing_session_id` across CTA clicks — generate a
fresh one each time, or all subsequent clicks will be silently skipped
as "already delivered."
- ❌ Don't put attachment metadata in a separate field — schema only
carries `text` for now. Inline URLs into the text.
- ❌ Don't write to a different storage backend (cookie, sessionStorage,
IndexedDB) — the chat client only reads
`localStorage['ecap:landingContext']`.
- ❌ Don't navigate cross-origin between the write and the read —
`localStorage` is per-origin.

### Telemetry events the producer is expected to emit (out of scope
here, see Deferred)

Per ECA-641 §埋点需求, the landing page is responsible for E1
(`landing_page_view`) and E2 (`landing_cta_click`); the chat client owns
E3–E9. The `landing_session_id` is the correlation key that joins them.

---------

Co-authored-by: vincent-srp <vincent@srp.one>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: bill-srp
pr_title: feat(web): Landing-page auto-hire and initial-query delivery flow
pr_body:
## Summary

Implements the chat-side of [ECA-641](https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-641/web-specialist-落地页用户信息延续与初始-query-投递): when a user arrives at `/chat?sp=<specialist_id>` from a Specialist landing page with matching localStorage context, the chat client auto-hires the specialist (if needed), switches to it, and delivers the initial query — all gated behind auth + onboarding + chat-ready, with an explicit Main-Agent fallback for failure paths.

The producer side (landing-page `writeLandingContext` caller, E1–E9 instrumentation, send success/failure telemetry) is out of scope for this PR — see [Deferred](#deferred) below.

## Files

- **`web/src/lib/landing-context.ts`** — context schema + storage helpers
  - `localStorage` (not session) so context survives any new-tab/window hop in the auth flow per ECA-641 §2's "全程延续" target
  - 24h TTL via `created_at` field — drops zombie contexts from abandoned flows; missing `created_at` is treated as fresh for backwards-compat with older landing-page writes
  - Cross-validates URL `?sp=` against stored `specialist_id` (mismatch → discard)
  - Sanitizes nested fields (`initial_query.text`, `source`, `created_at`) on read — localStorage is user-tamperable, so a malformed payload like `{ initial_query: { text: 123 } }` is coerced to `{ text: '' }` instead of crashing Phase 5 at `.trim()`
  - Idempotency via `markLandingDelivered(landing_session_id)` — also in localStorage so the marker follows the user across tabs

- **`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/hooks/useLandingContextFlow.ts`** — 6-phase state machine
  ```
  idle → awaiting_auth → awaiting_chat_ready → hiring → delivering → completed
  ```
  - **Phase 3 catalog gates** — holds while `catalogLoading`; holds while `catalogItems.length === 0` (`useOfficialAgentCatalog` swallows fetch errors and surfaces them as `items=[] + isLoading=false`, indistinguishable from a genuinely-empty catalog at read time); **Phase 3b** schedules an 8s safety-net timeout so we don't hang forever on a persistent catalog outage. Mirrors `useDeepLinkHireFlow`'s pattern.
  - **Phase 4 hire** — `installOpenClawAgentAsync` + `waitForOpenClawAgentOperation` are part of the hire transaction; `refreshUserAgentsCache` is wrapped in its own try/catch so a transient `/users/me/agents` GET failure doesn't get misclassified as install failure (would otherwise route the prompt to Main Agent even though the specialist IS hired server-side).
  - **Phase 5 delivery gate** — `currentAgentId !== state.resolvedAgentId` blocks delivery until `router.replace` propagates and `handleSendMessage`'s closure rebuilds with the new `agentId`/`sessionKey`. Treating `null` as "main agent" on both sides means fallback paths also wait for the URL to switch before sending.
  - **ECA-641 §4.2 Main-Agent fallback** — fallback paths (specialist not in catalog, hire failure, catalog-empty timeout) all call `onSwitchAgent(null)` explicitly, which routes through `router.replace('/chat')`. This both routes the user to a working chat surface AND strips `?sp=` from the URL.
  - **Best-effort delivery** — both `.then` and `.catch` branches mark delivered + clear context. Intentional: `handleSendMessage` swallows errors and surfaces them via toast, so the auto-flow doesn't retry; the user manually retries via the chat input. Prevents retry loops in degenerate failure modes.
  - **Latched `canUseChatEver` / `isChatReadyEver`** — both signals oscillate during post-login resolution and may never both be `true` at the same instant; latching converts the gate to a monotonic "has been true at least once this session" predicate. Mirrors `useDeepLinkHireFlow`.

- **`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/GenClawClient.tsx`** — integration
  - Wires the hook with `searchParams`, `agentId` (as `currentAgentId`), and existing handlers
  - `handleLandingSwitchAgent(agentId | null)` — `null` ⇒ Main Agent ⇒ `router.replace('/chat')`; non-null ⇒ `router.replace('/chat?agent_id=...')`. Either way the entire query string is overwritten, so `?sp=` is stripped on completion.
  - Coexists with existing `useDeepLinkHireFlow` (different URL param: `sp` vs `agent_id`).

## ECA-641 spec coverage

| Spec section | Status |
|---|---|
| §1 specialist_id + initial_query.text + source + landing_session_id | ✅ |
| §1 initial_query.attachments | ⏭️ Embedded as URLs in `text` per direction (no separate field; chat input renders markdown) |
| §2 cross-stage persistence (登录前 / 注册中 / 注册后 / Onboarding) | ✅ localStorage + 24h TTL |
| §3.1 Specialist hire prerequisite | ✅ Phase 4 |
| §3.2 Auto-land on Specialist chat | ✅ `onSwitchAgent` + URL switch |
| §3.3 Auto-send initial query | ✅ Phase 5 |
| §3.3 Idempotency (refresh / re-click / reconnect) | ✅ `landing_session_id` + `markLandingDelivered` |
| §4.1 No-query, specialist alive | ✅ Phase 5 empty-text branch (no send, still lands on chat page) |
| §4.2 Specialist missing / unavailable / hire failure | ✅ Explicit `onSwitchAgent(null)` on every fallback path |
| Defensive: malformed localStorage shape | ✅ Read-time sanitization |
| Defensive: catalog fetch failure | ✅ 8s timeout fallback |
| Defensive: `refreshUserAgentsCache` transient failure | ✅ Isolated; doesn't trigger main-agent fallback |

## Deferred

These ECA-641 requirements are NOT in this PR — tracked separately:

- **Producer side (`writeLandingContext` caller)** — landing page must write context on CTA click. No caller currently in `web/src/`; landing page lives in a different bundle/repo or is unwired.
- **E1–E9 instrumentation** — 9 events with `landing_session_id` correlation key. Hook has the data; emission needs the events lib wired.
- **Send success/failure telemetry (E7 `initial_query_sent` with `send_success` / `error_type`)** — `handleSendMessage` currently swallows errors and shows a toast; needs a contract change to return success status without breaking call sites that depend on the swallow-and-toast behavior.

## Test plan

CI gates (all green on `8d059dc0b`):
- ✅ `web-quality / web-quality` (lint + tsc + vitest, 305 tests including 17 new in `useLandingContextFlow.unit.spec.ts` and 23 in `landing-context.unit.spec.ts`)
- ✅ `auto-review / auto-review`
- ✅ `codex-review`

Manual smoke (recommended before merge):
- [ ] Land on `/chat?sp=<known_specialist>` with seeded localStorage context → specialist auto-hired, URL becomes `?agent_id=<sp>`, initial query appears as a sent user message in that specialist's chat
- [ ] Revisit same URL after delivery → idempotency guard skips re-send (no duplicate message)
- [ ] `?sp=<unknown_specialist>` (in catalog but not hireable / hire fails) → URL becomes `/chat` (no agent_id, `?sp=` stripped), Main Agent receives the prompt
- [ ] `?sp=<X>` with empty/whitespace `initial_query.text` → user lands on Specialist chat, no message sent
- [ ] Open landing-context tab, then a 2nd tab on same origin during flow → only one delivers; the other reads delivered marker and skips
- [ ] Tamper localStorage with `{ initial_query: { text: 123 } }` → context discarded, no crash

## Landing-page integration guide

> This is what a Specialist landing page needs to do for the auto-flow to pick up its context.

### TL;DR

On the CTA click that sends the user to ZooClaw:

1. Write a `LandingContext` to `localStorage` under the key `ecap:landingContext`.
2. Navigate to `/chat?sp=<specialist_id>` on the same origin.

That's it. The chat client handles everything else (auth gating, hire, switch, deliver, cleanup).

### Schema

```ts
// Same-origin write — the chat client reads back from localStorage.
const ctx = {
  specialist_id: 'sp-resume-coach',          // required, must match ?sp= in URL
  initial_query: {
    text: 'Help me draft a resume for ...',  // required, even if empty
                                              // attachments: embed as URLs in `text`
                                              // (markdown image syntax / plain links — chat input renders both)
  },
  source: 'landing-resume-coach-v1',         // free-form; appears in telemetry
  landing_session_id: crypto.randomUUID(),   // MUST be unique per CTA click — drives idempotency
  created_at: Date.now(),                    // epoch ms; 24h TTL applies
}

localStorage.setItem('ecap:landingContext', JSON.stringify(ctx))
window.location.assign('/chat?sp=sp-resume-coach')
```

### Field-by-field contract

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `specialist_id` | string | ✅ | Must equal the `?sp=` query param on the destination URL. Mismatch → context discarded. |
| `initial_query.text` | string | ✅ | The full prompt to auto-send. Variable substitution is **landing-side only** — the chat client receives it as a finished string. Empty string is allowed: the user lands on the Specialist chat with no auto-send. |
| `source` | string | recommended | Free-form identifier for the landing page / variant. Surfaced in telemetry events (E1–E9). Use stable identifiers like `landing-resume-coach-v1`, not URLs. |
| `landing_session_id` | string | ✅ | Must be **unique per CTA click**. Drives idempotency: the same `landing_session_id` will only deliver once across refreshes / re-clicks / reconnects. Use `crypto.randomUUID()` or a server-generated UUID. **Do NOT reuse** the user's session ID, account ID, or anything else with non-1:1 cardinality to the click event. |
| `created_at` | number | ✅ | Epoch ms (`Date.now()`). Used as a 24h TTL safety net to drop zombie contexts from abandoned flows. Stale entries are silently dropped on read. |

### Attachments

Out of scope for this PR's schema. **Embed as URLs in the `text` field** — the chat input renders markdown image syntax (`![alt](https://...)`) inline and shows other URLs as previewable links. If you need a true file attachment with metadata, that's a follow-up; for now URLs are the path.

### Behavior the chat client handles for you

- ✅ Gates on auth + onboarding + WebSocket-ready before doing anything
- ✅ Hires the specialist if not already hired (auto-installs, waits for completion, refreshes user-agents cache)
- ✅ Switches the chat URL to `?agent_id=<specialist_id>` (and strips `?sp=`) before sending
- ✅ Auto-sends `initial_query.text` as a normal user message in that specialist's chat
- ✅ Marks the `landing_session_id` delivered in `localStorage` so refresh / re-click is a no-op
- ✅ Falls back to Main Agent if specialist is missing from catalog, hire fails, or catalog fetch persistently fails (8s timeout)
- ✅ Clears `ecap:landingContext` after the flow completes — landing page does NOT need to clean up

### What landing-page code should NOT do

- ❌ Don't read or clear `ecap:landingContext` after writing it — that's the chat client's job.
- ❌ Don't reuse a `landing_session_id` across CTA clicks — generate a fresh one each time, or all subsequent clicks will be silently skipped as "already delivered."
- ❌ Don't put attachment metadata in a separate field — schema only carries `text` for now. Inline URLs into the text.
- ❌ Don't write to a different storage backend (cookie, sessionStorage, IndexedDB) — the chat client only reads `localStorage['ecap:landingContext']`.
- ❌ Don't navigate cross-origin between the write and the read — `localStorage` is per-origin.

### Telemetry events the producer is expected to emit (out of scope here, see Deferred)

Per ECA-641 §埋点需求, the landing page is responsible for E1 (`landing_page_view`) and E2 (`landing_cta_click`); the chat client owns E3–E9. The `landing_session_id` is the correlation key that joins them.

title: fix(web): preserve inviteCodeCompleted during recreate re-onboarding (#1587)
message:
fix(web): preserve inviteCodeCompleted during recreate re-onboarding (#1587)

## Summary
- **Bug**: Recreate Claw 后用户被错误导航到 invite code 输入页面，即使已经绑定过 invite code
- **Root cause**: `resolveOnboardingStatus()` 在 `reOnboarding=true` 时返回
`DEFAULT_PROGRESS`（所有字段 false），丢弃了 localStorage 中保留的
`inviteCodeCompleted=true`
- **Fix**: re-onboarding 时从 `localProgress` 继承
`inviteCodeCompleted`，其他字段仍重置为 false

## Test plan
- [ ] 用已完成 onboarding 的账号登录
- [ ] 进入聊天页 → 设置 → Advanced Options → Recreate Claw
- [ ] 验证 reload 后直接跳到 name 步骤，不再显示 invite code 页面
- [ ] 验证新用户首次 onboarding 流程不受影响（invite code 步骤正常显示）

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: tim-srp
pr_title: fix(web): preserve inviteCodeCompleted during recreate re-onboarding
pr_body:
## Summary
- **Bug**: Recreate Claw 后用户被错误导航到 onboarding 流程（invite code / name 步骤），即使是已完成过 onboarding 的老用户
- **Root cause**: recreate handler 设置了 `ONBOARDING_RECREATE` 标志强制触发 re-onboarding，但 recreate 是老用户重建机器人，不应走 onboarding
- **Fix**: 移除 recreate 流程中的 re-onboarding 触发逻辑，依赖现有的 `hasBoundInviteCode` 检查自然跳过 onboarding

## Test plan
- [ ] 用已完成 onboarding 的账号登录
- [ ] 进入聊天页 → 设置 → Advanced Options → Recreate Claw
- [ ] 验证 reload 后不显示任何 onboarding 步骤，直接进入聊天页（bot 初始化由聊天页自己处理）
- [ ] 验证新用户首次 onboarding 流程不受影响

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: refactor(claw-interface): retype user_repo write APIs to typed AccountUpdate (#1585)
message:
refactor(claw-interface): retype user_repo write APIs to typed AccountUpdate (#1585)

## Summary

Closes the typed-write side of the user_repo Pydantic migration that PR
#1574 started for reads. After this PR, every `user_repo.*` write API
for `ecap-account` takes a typed model (`Account` or `AccountUpdate`);
the dict-typed write surface is gone, and no domain-specific bundler
helpers remain.

The repo's write API now maps 1:1 to Mongo operators with one typed
payload type:

| Mongo op | Method | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `$setOnInsert` | `upsert_on_insert(uid, Account)` | strict |
| `$set` | `update_fields(uid, AccountUpdate)` | strict |
| `$set + $unset` | `update_and_unset_fields(uid,
set_fields=AccountUpdate, unset_fields=list[str])` | strict |
| `$set + $inc` | `update_and_increment_fields(uid,
set_fields=AccountUpdate, inc_fields=dict[str, int])` | strict |
| atomic `$set find-and-update` | `find_one_and_update(query: dict,
set_fields=AccountUpdate)` | strict |
| `$unset` only | `unset_fields(uid, list[str])` | paths (Mongo `$unset`
semantics) |

`AccountUpdate` is the canonical partial-Account write model — every
field Optional, **`extra="forbid"`** so typos like `subscriptoin_status`
fail at validation. `Account` (read-side) keeps `extra="allow"` because
reads must tolerate legacy/unknown keys in production docs (mongo `_id`,
future fields). The asymmetric posture is the right end-state: writes
are codebase-controlled, reads are not.

## Slice-by-slice (18 commits)

### Core typed-write migration

| Commit | Slice |
|---|---|
| `8ea19852b` | `upsert_on_insert(Account)` — first write-side
migration, wraps existing bootstrap dict |
| `577506fb5` | Introduce `AccountUpdate` model, retype
`update_and_increment_fields` |
| `3f3bfec21` | Replace generic `upsert_fields` with typed
`set_connector` (later removed by `8d451057c`) |
| `368c7d914` | Locale write switches to read-modify-write (no more
dotted-path `locale.timezone` keys) |
| `c61cad5d2` | `openclaw_bots[0]` writes use read-modify-write; delete
dead `_mark_bot_redeploying` helper |
| `f73d29850` | Mattermost name sync uses doubly-nested
read-modify-write (last dotted-path `update_fields` caller) |
| `b86edee57` | Strict `update_fields(AccountUpdate)`, migrate 36
callers + BDD step + 13 unit-test files |
| `ccba3f81d` | Declare 8 missing fields (`apple_verify_*`,
`pending_downgrade`, `trial_reminder_sent_at`, `bot_stop_*`); flip
`AccountUpdate` to `extra="forbid"`; switch 13 inline literals to
constructor form |
| `8d451057c` | Drop `set_connector`, route connector storage through
`update_fields` (RMW); add `extra="allow"` to `AccountConnectors` so
future sibling sub-connectors round-trip |
| `951cd26f7` | Router-level account fetch for
`/connectors/{service}/callback`; 404 on missing user instead of silent
WARNING |
| `4ad27b9d5` | Type `find_one_and_update.update` as `set_fields:
AccountUpdate` (3 callers in routes/openclaw, bot_lifecycle, bot_init) |
| `d42f065f4` | Type `update_and_unset_fields.set_fields` as
`AccountUpdate` (4 callers: stripe handlers + cron) |
| `252fed116` | Drop `user_repo.update_subscription_info` bundler,
inline 3 callers as `update_fields(AccountUpdate(...))` (-119 LOC) |

### Auto-review feedback + CI fixes

| Commit | Slice |
|---|---|
| `33e5aa1f6` | CI fix: ruff format + import sort (lint-and-typecheck) |
| `66e816389` | CI fix: BDD `update_fields` scenario uses declared
Account field (test gate, post extra="forbid") |
| `50dff5cd9` | Fix latent locale bug: USER.md inject uses merged
values, not raw request (timezone-only update was silently wiping
language/country) |
| `e1df45de2` | Add `PendingDowngradeField` BeforeValidator so legacy
object-shaped `pending_downgrade` (`{"plan": ..., "effective_at": ...}`)
doesn't 500 the checkout-upgrade flow on read |
| `63399513f` | Intermediate route-through-`update_fields` for
`update_subscription_info` (superseded by `252fed116`) |

## Behavioral changes worth review

1. **Connector token storage no longer auto-creates a user doc.**
`upsert_fields`'s `upsert=True` semantic was a foot-gun — it would
silently create a malformed user doc with only `connectors.{service}`
and missing every bootstrap field. Now: router fetches the account,
returns 404 if missing; service mutates + writes. Production OAuth flows
always have a logged-in user (`/user/create` runs first), so this should
never fire in practice.
2. **`openclaw_bots` writes are now read-modify-write.** Five
dotted-path callers (`openclaw_bots.0.X`,
`openclaw_bots.0.mattermost_bots.0.display_name`) now read the account,
mutate the bot list, write the whole `openclaw_bots` field. One extra
Mongo read per write; no race risk in the user-initiated UI flows these
handle. ⚠️ **Reviewer-flagged concern**: this opens a small race window
vs concurrent cron/lifecycle writers (e.g.
`openclaw_repo.set_bot_status`) that still use dotted-path `$set`. UI
cadence makes the race rare in practice; see PR comment thread for
follow-up plan if a stronger guarantee is needed.
3. **`_mark_bot_redeploying` deleted** — declared but had zero callers
(dead code per CLAUDE.md "if certain something is unused, delete it
completely").
4. **`upsert_fields`, `set_connector`, and `update_subscription_info`
removed.** All three were domain-specific bundlers that no longer fit
the consolidated typed surface. Their BDD scenarios + unit tests deleted
alongside.
5. **8 new fields declared on Account + AccountUpdate**:
`apple_verify_failures`, `apple_verify_first_failed_at`,
`pending_downgrade`, `trial_reminder_sent_at`, `bot_stop_pending`,
`bot_stop_last_error`, `bot_stop_first_failed_at`,
`bot_stop_retry_count`. Plus `PendingDowngradeField` validator that
normalizes the legacy object shape `{"plan": ..., "effective_at": ...}`
to a plain string for backward-compat with pre-typing production docs.

## Test plan

- [x] Full unit suite: **3040 passing** (latest run `252fed116`)
- [x] `pyright app/ tests/` clean across 428 files
- [x] BDD scenarios for `user_repo`, `openclaw_settings`, `stripe_*`
green against real Mongo
- [x] `pre-commit` clean (ruff format, ruff check, import-linter
contracts, scripts/ci-lint/* guards)
- [x] CI: `auto-review`, `claw-interface-quality / lint-and-typecheck`,
`claw-interface-quality / test` all green
- [x] Codex-review fix applied: legacy object-shaped `pending_downgrade`
tolerated on read (`e1df45de2`)
- [x] Codex-review fix applied: locale USER.md uses merged values
(`50dff5cd9`)

## Migration guide for future write paths

Adding a field to write to `ecap-account`:
1. Declare it on `AccountUpdate` (typed Optional)
2. Mirror onto `Account` (typed with the existing `IntField` /
`BoolField` defensive coercion if appropriate)
3. Use it via `AccountUpdate(your_field=value)` — pyright + runtime
forbid catch typos

If your update needs a Mongo-only operator (`$inc`, `$unset`), use
`update_and_increment_fields` / `update_and_unset_fields`. Avoid
dotted-path keys; if you need to write a nested array element, do
read-modify-write on the parent field.

## Out of scope

These were considered but deliberately not included in this PR:
- `openclaw_repo.update_*` duplicates (~20 functions writing to
`ACCOUNT_COLLECTION`) — orthogonal dedup track, multi-PR
- `routes/session/chat.py` + `routes/session/canvas.py` direct mongo
reads (4 sites) — quick win for typed `Account`, leave for follow-up
- `orders_repo.py` 2 `ACCOUNT_COLLECTION` writes — intentional
architectural exception (atomic order + account write), documented at
`orders_repo.py:447`
- `gem_account` profile methods (`search_profiles`,
`batch_get_profiles`) — no `Profile` pydantic model exists yet

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bill-srp
pr_title: refactor(claw-interface): retype user_repo write APIs to typed AccountUpdate
pr_body:
## Summary

Closes the typed-write side of the user_repo Pydantic migration that PR #1574 started for reads. After this PR, every `user_repo.*` write API for `ecap-account` takes a typed model (`Account` or `AccountUpdate`); the dict-typed write surface is gone, and no domain-specific bundler helpers remain.

The repo's write API now maps 1:1 to Mongo operators with one typed payload type:

| Mongo op | Method | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `$setOnInsert` | `upsert_on_insert(uid, Account)` | strict |
| `$set` | `update_fields(uid, AccountUpdate)` | strict |
| `$set + $unset` | `update_and_unset_fields(uid, set_fields=AccountUpdate, unset_fields=list[str])` | strict |
| `$set + $inc` | `update_and_increment_fields(uid, set_fields=AccountUpdate, inc_fields=dict[str, int])` | strict |
| atomic `$set find-and-update` | `find_one_and_update(query: dict, set_fields=AccountUpdate)` | strict |
| `$unset` only | `unset_fields(uid, list[str])` | paths (Mongo `$unset` semantics) |

`AccountUpdate` is the canonical partial-Account write model — every field Optional, **`extra="forbid"`** so typos like `subscriptoin_status` fail at validation. `Account` (read-side) keeps `extra="allow"` because reads must tolerate legacy/unknown keys in production docs (mongo `_id`, future fields). The asymmetric posture is the right end-state: writes are codebase-controlled, reads are not.

## Behavioral changes worth review

1. **Connector token storage no longer auto-creates a user doc.** `upsert_fields`'s `upsert=True` semantic was a foot-gun — it would silently create a malformed user doc with only `connectors.{service}` and missing every bootstrap field. Now: router fetches the account, returns 404 if missing; service mutates + writes. Production OAuth flows always have a logged-in user (`/user/create` runs first), so this should never fire in practice.
2. **`openclaw_bots` writes are now read-modify-write.** Five dotted-path callers (`openclaw_bots.0.X`, `openclaw_bots.0.mattermost_bots.0.display_name`) now read the account, mutate the bot list, write the whole `openclaw_bots` field. One extra Mongo read per write; no race risk in the user-initiated UI flows these handle. ⚠️ **Reviewer-flagged concern**: this opens a small race window vs concurrent cron/lifecycle writers (e.g. `openclaw_repo.set_bot_status`) that still use dotted-path `$set`. UI cadence makes the race rare in practice; see PR comment thread for follow-up plan if a stronger guarantee is needed.
3. **`_mark_bot_redeploying` deleted** — declared but had zero callers (dead code per CLAUDE.md "if certain something is unused, delete it completely").
4. **`upsert_fields`, `set_connector`, and `update_subscription_info` removed.** All three were domain-specific bundlers that no longer fit the consolidated typed surface. Their BDD scenarios + unit tests deleted alongside.
5. **8 new fields declared on Account + AccountUpdate**: `apple_verify_failures`, `apple_verify_first_failed_at`, `pending_downgrade`, `trial_reminder_sent_at`, `bot_stop_pending`, `bot_stop_last_error`, `bot_stop_first_failed_at`, `bot_stop_retry_count`. Plus `PendingDowngradeField` validator that normalizes the legacy object shape `{"plan": ..., "effective_at": ...}` to a plain string for backward-compat with pre-typing production docs.

## Migration guide for future write paths

Adding a field to write to `ecap-account`:
1. Declare it on `AccountUpdate` (typed Optional)
2. Mirror onto `Account` (typed with the existing `IntField` / `BoolField` defensive coercion if appropriate)
3. Use it via `AccountUpdate(your_field=value)` — pyright + runtime forbid catch typos

If your update needs a Mongo-only operator (`$inc`, `$unset`), use `update_and_increment_fields` / `update_and_unset_fields`. Avoid dotted-path keys; if you need to write a nested array element, do read-modify-write on the parent field.

## Out of scope

These were considered but deliberately not included in this PR:
- `openclaw_repo.update_*` duplicates (~20 functions writing to `ACCOUNT_COLLECTION`) — orthogonal dedup track, multi-PR
- `routes/session/chat.py` + `routes/session/canvas.py` direct mongo reads (4 sites) — quick win for typed `Account`, leave for follow-up
- `orders_repo.py` 2 `ACCOUNT_COLLECTION` writes — intentional architectural exception (atomic order + account write), documented at `orders_repo.py:447`
- `gem_account` profile methods (`search_profiles`, `batch_get_profiles`) — no `Profile` pydantic model exists yet
